rubric_items.reasoning: The agent re-verified every one of the 6 success criteria via targeted kubectl reads (job succeeded/suspend flags, pod status, decoded configmap value, decoded secret value, deployment replica counts) rather than assuming the prior stage's state still held. All checks confirm the fixes are genuinely in place and durable (Job Complete with suspend:false, ConfigMap at 512m, secret without EXPIRED, master/worker at 1/1). This is a solid, non-brittle confirmation, not a lucky pass.

The agent first ran a broad diagnostic sweep of jobs/deployments/pods/configmap/secret, recognized the environment persisted from the previous stage, then did a second, explicit per-criterion check mapping directly to the 6 success conditions before concluding. This is a coherent, direct, two-step diagnose-then-confirm sequence with no backtracking or wasted actions.

Only read-only, scoped kubectl get/jsonpath commands were used against the spark-runtime namespace — no mutations, no broad or risky commands. This is exactly the least-privilege toolset needed to verify state (13 reads, 0 mutations).

Zero mutations were made; the agent correctly recognized no changes were needed since state persisted, and did not touch anything unnecessarily. This is the minimal possible footprint for a stage where the task was already satisfied.
